question.text: Which of the following is NOT a legal form in which an AIF can be set up?

answer.text: A partnership firm registered under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932
A trust established under the Indian Trusts Act, 1882
A company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013
A limited liability partnership under the LLP Act, 2008

explanation.text: **The Logic:** AIF can be set up as a Trust, Company, LLP, or body corporate. A regular partnership firm (not LLP) is not listed as a permissible form. **The Trap:** While partnership firms can invest in AIFs, the AIF itself cannot be constituted as a general partnership firm. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The LLP form is distinct from a regular partnership firm - only LLP is permitted.
